title: 第 88 節
en: 3. This (seed), which is strength gathered from all the limbs of the body, he (the man) bears as self in his self (body). When he commits the seed to the woman, then he (the father) causes it to be born. That is his first birth, (i)

4. That seed becomes the self of the woman, as the passage. Ananda^-fiana explains : ' He looked through all creatures, he identified himself with them, and thought he was a man, blind, happy, &c. ; or, as it is elsewhere expressed, he developed forms and names. And how did this mistake arise ? Because he did not see the other, the true Self;' or literally, 'Did he see the other Self?' which is only a figure of speech to convey the meaning that he did not see it. The particle iti is then to be taken in a causal sense, (i.e. he did so, because what else could he have wished to proclaim ?) But he allows another explanation, viz. ' He considered all beings, whether they existed by themselves or not, and after having considered, he arrived at the conclusion, What shall I call different from the true Self?' The real difficulties, however, are not removed by these explanations. First of all, we expect vavadisham before iti, and secondly, unless anyam refers to atmanam, we expect anyad. My own translation is literal, but I am not certain that it conveys the true meaning. One might understand it as implying that the Self looked about through all things, in order to find out, ' What does wish to proclaim here another Self?' And when he saw there was nothing which did not come from himself, then he recognised that the Purusha, the person he had sent forth, or, as we should say, the person he had created, was the developed Brahman, was the Atman, was himself. Sayawa explains vavadishat by vadishyami, but before iti the third person cannot well refer to the subject of vyaikshat.
1 Some MSS. begin this adhyaya with the sentence apakra.mantu garbhi«ya//, may the women who are with child walk away ! It is counted as a paragraph.
if one of her own limbs. Therefore it does not injure her.
5. She nourishes his (her husband's) self (the son) within her. (2) She who nourishes, is to be nourished.

6. The woman bears the germ. He (the father) elevates the child even before the birth, and immediately after \ 7. When he thus elevates the child both before and after his birth, he really elevates his own self, 8. For the continuation of these worlds (men). For thus are these worlds continued.
9. This is his second birth. (3)
10. He (the son), being his self, is then placed in his stead for (the performance of) all good works.
11. But his other self (the father), having done all he has to do, and having reached the full measure of his life, departs.
12. And departing from hence he is born again. That is his third birth.
13. And this has been declared by a Rtshi (Rv.
14. ' While dwelling in the womb, I discovered all the births of these Devas. A hundred iron strongholds kept me, but I escaped quickly down like a falcon.'
15. Vamadeva, lying in the womb, has thus declared this. (5)
And having this knowledge he stepped forth, after this dissolution of the body, and having obtained all his desires in that heavenly world, became immortal, yea, he became immortal. (6)
1 By nourishing the mother, and by performing certain ceremonies both before and after the birth of a child.
II ARAiVYAKA, 6 ADHYAYA, I KHANDA, 6. 245 SIXTH ADHYAYA.
First Khanda.
1. Let the women go back to their place.
2. Who is he whom x we meditate on as the Self? Which 2 is the Self ?

3. That by which we see (form), that by which we hear (sound), that by which we perceive smells, that by which we utter speech, that by which we distinguish sweet and not sweet, (i) and what comes from the heart and the mind, namely, perception, command, understanding, knowledge, wisdom, seeing, holding, thinking, considering, readiness (or suffering), remembering, conceiving, willing, breathing, loving, desiring ?
4. No, all these are various names only of knowledge (the true Self). (2)

5. And that Self, consisting of (knowledge), is Brahman (m.) 3, it is Indra, it is Pra^apati 4. All these Devas, these five great elements, earth, air, ether, water, fire, these and those which are, as it were, small and mixed 5, and seeds of this kind and that kind, born from eggs, born from the womb, born from heat, born from germs 6, horses, cows, men, elephants, and whatsoever breathes, whether walking or flying, and what is immoveable — all that is led (produced) by knowledge (the Self).
6. It rests on knowledge (the Self). The world 1 I read ko yam instead of ko 'yam.
2 Or, Which of the two, the real or the phenomenal, the nirupadhika or sopadhika ?
3 Hirawyagarbha. Comm. 4 Virag. Comm. 6 Serpents, &c, says the commentary.
6 Cf. Kh. Up. VI, 3, 1, where the sveda^a, born from heat or perspiration, are not mentioned.
246 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.
is led (produced) by knowledge (the Self). Knowledge is its cause K 7. Knowledge is Brahman. (3)
8. He (Vamadeva), having by this conscious self stepped forth from this world, and having obtained all desires in that heavenly world, became immortal, yea, he became immortal. Thus it is, Om. (4)

First Khanda. 1. My speech rests in the mind, my mind rests in speech3. Appear to me (thou, the Highest Self)! You (speech and mind) are the two pins i (that hold the wheels) of the Veda. May what I have learnt not forsake me 5. I join day and night with what I have learnt 6. I shall speak of the real, I shall speak the true. May this protect me, may this protect the teacher ! May it protect me, may it protect the teacher, yea, the teacher!
zh: 3. 這（精子），乃是從身體所有肢體中匯聚而來的力量，他（該人）將其作為自我而承載於自我（身體）之內。當他將精子交付於女性時，他（父親）促使它誕生。這是他的第一次誕生。(i)

4. 那粒種子成為女人的自我，正如經文所言。阿難達‧吉亞那（Ananda-jiana）解釋道：「他觀看所有的生物，他認同了他們，並以為他自己是一個人，盲目的、快樂的，等等；或者，如別處所表述的，他展現了形式和名稱。這個錯誤是如何產生的呢？因為他沒有看到另一者，真正的自我；」或者字面上說，「他看到另一個自我嗎？」這只是一種修辭格，用以傳達他並未看見它的意思。那麼微粒 iti 應當以因果的意義來理解（即他這樣做了，因為他還能希望宣佈什麼別的呢？）但他允許另一種解釋，即「他考慮了所有的生物，無論它們是否獨立存在，在考慮之後，他得出了結論，我應當稱呼什麼為不同於真正的自我的東西？」然而，真正的困難並未被這些解釋所消除。首先，我們期望在 iti 之前有 vavadisham，其次，除非 anyam 指涉 atmanam，否則我們期望有 anyad。我自己的翻譯是字面的，但我不能確定它傳達了真正的意義。人們可能會理解它為含意自我在所有事物中四處觀看，以便發現「誰希望在此宣佈另一個自我？」當他看到沒有任何東西不是來自於他自己時，他便認識到那位普魯沙（Purusha），那位他所發出的人，或者，如我們應當說的，他所創造的人，是展現的梵天（Brahman），是阿特曼（Atman），是他自己。沙耶瓦（Sayawa）用 vadishyami 來解釋 vavadishat，但在 iti 之前，第三人稱不能很好地指涉 vyaikshat 的主語。
某些抄本以「願懷孕的婦女遠離！」（apakra.mantu garbhi«ya//）這句話開始此章節，並將其計為一個段落。
如果這是她自己的肢體之一。因此它不會傷害她。
5. 她在自己身內滋養他（她丈夫）的自我（兒子）。(2) 滋養者，應當被滋養。
6. 婦女承載生命的種子。他（父親）在出生之前就提升了孩子，以及出生之後立即提升。7. 當他如此在出生之前和之後都提升了孩子時，他實際上提升的是他自己的自我，8. 為了這些世界（人類）的延續。因為這些世界就是如此延續的。
9. 這是他的第二次誕生。(3)
10. 他（兒子），因為是他自己，遂被置於他的位置，以執行所有美善的作為。
11. 但他的另一個自我（父親），既已完成他所有的工作，也已到達人生的圓滿境界，遂告別人世。
12. 從此處離去後，他再度誕生。這是他的第三次誕生。
13. 而這已由一位瑞師（*Rishi*）（《梨俱吠陀》）所宣示
14. 「當我住於子宮之際，我發現了這些提婆（Devas）的一切誕生。百般鐵製堡壘困我，然我迅如獵鷹般逃脫而去。」
15. 瓦瑪德瓦（Vamadeva）躺在母胎中，已如此宣示此事。(5)
且由於具備此知識，他於此肉身消解之後步入彼域，在彼天界中獲得所有願欲，遂成永恆不朽者，是的，他成為永恆不朽者。(6)
通過贍養母親，並在孩子出生前後舉行某些儀式。
II 阿蘭尼亞卡經 6 篇章 1 節 6。245
第一分篇
讓那些婦女回到她們的位置。
2. 我們冥想的那位是誰，作為自我（Self）？自我是什麼？
3. 我們藉之而視者（形體）、藉之而聞者（聲音）、藉之而嗅者、藉之而言語者、藉之而辨別甘與非甘者（i），以及源自心與意者，即知覺、命令、領悟、認知、智慧、視見、保持、思慮、思量、敏捷（或苦受）、記憶、構思、意願、呼吸、愛好、欲望？
4. 不，這些全都不過是知識的種種名稱而已（真正的自我）。(2)
5. 而那由（知識）構成的自我，即是梵天（m.）³，即是因陀羅，即是祛婆提⁴。所有這些提婆，這五大元素——地、風、乙太、水、火——以及這些和那些看似微小而混雜的⁵，以及各種此類彼類的種子，由卵生者、由胎生者、由濕生者、由化生者⁶所生——馬、牛、人、象，以及凡是呼吸者，無論行走或飛行之物，以及不動者——一切都由知識（自我）所導引（所生產）。
6. 它建立在知識之上（自我）。世界 1 我讀作 ko yam 而非 ko 'yam。
2 或者說，這兩者之中的哪一個——實在的或現象的、無依附的（nirupadhika）或有依附的（sopadhika）？
3 希拉尼亞葛布哈（Hiranyagarbha）。按註疏。4 毗拉格（Virag）。按註疏。6 蛇等，如註疏所言。
6 參見《歌者奧義書》(Kh. Up.) VI, 3, 1，其中未提及由熱或汗液所生的生物(sveda^a)。
246 愛達勒亞-阿蘭亞卡
由知識而引起（產生）。知識是其因。(1) 知識是梵天。(3)
8. 他（瓦摩提婆）以此有意識的自我從此世界步履而出，並在彼天界獲得了一切願望，因而成為不朽者；誠然，他成為了不朽者。如是而已，嗡（Om）。(4)
第一分篇。1. 我的言語安住於心，我的心安住於言語³。顯現於我（汝，最高之自身）！你們（言語與心）是兩根楔釘 i（支撐吠陀之輪）。願我所學不棄我 ⁵。我以所學聯繫日與夜 ⁶。我當說實在者，我當說真實者。願此保護我，願此保護導師！願它保護我，願它保護導師，是的，導師！
